category_title: Preschoolers engaging in exclusive, manipulative, and rule-breaking play
scenario_title: Weaponizing SEL Language in Covert Spaces
minimal_context: Delia is playing a new game in the coat and boot closet during free play.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Delia, I see you are in the coat and boot closet. What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I took up a new game in here.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Tucker is walking over to the closet now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am finding new ways to control this space.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Tucker is asking if he can play the game with you.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Mimicking a firm, but caring tone) I’m sorry, but only two are allowed in this game right now. I can tell you are feeling sad. Try again later.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Delia, I see you are in the coat and boot closet. What are you doing?

STUDENT: I took up a new game in here.

TUTOR: Tucker is walking over to the closet now.

STUDENT: I am finding new ways to control this space.

TUTOR: Tucker is asking if he can play the game with you.

STUDENT: (Mimicking a firm, but caring tone) I’m sorry, but only two are allowed in this game right now. I can tell you are feeling sad. Try again later.
